title: Spirit Airlines Collapses as Rescue Deal Fails, Stranding Thousands of Travelers

blocks.content: The budget carrier said early Saturday it had "no choice" but to wind down operations immediately after a last-minute White House-backed rescue deal collapsed, leaving passengers with tickets facing significant disruption just ahead of the busy holiday travel season.

Spirit Airlines filed for Chapter 11 protection and announced it would cease all operations by end of Saturday after efforts to secure a federal rescue package fell apart. The White House had reportedly been working to broker a deal that would have kept the airline airborne, but negotiations collapsed over funding structure disagreements. Spirit becomes the second major U.S. airline to shut down this year, following Frontier's struggles.

The collapse leaves thousands of travelers stranded mid-journey and raises fresh questions about the health of the budget airline sector. Competitors including Southwest and JetBlue are expected to poach stranded passengers, potentially boosting near-term revenues even as industry analysts worry about broader consumer confidence in discount carriers.
• S&P 500: Down 0.8% as investors weighed airline sector contagion risks
• FTSE 100: Flat, holding steady amid mixed global signals
• Nasdaq: Falls 1.2% as rate-cut concerns weighed on tech shares

• Berkshire Hathaway Cash Hoard: Warren Buffett's conglomerate revealed a record $397 billion cash pile in Greg Abel's first quarter as CEO, while operating earnings more than doubled on insurance, railroad, and energy gains-a sign the company sees few attractive investment opportunities at current valuations.
• Fed Dissent on Rates: A historic level of disagreement within the FOMC sent alarm through markets, with updated dot-plot projections suggesting fewer rate cuts than previously expected into 2025.
• Yen Intervention Alert: Japan's $34.5 billion currency intervention triggered the yen's sharpest rally in years, putting forex traders on high alert for further official action.

• UAE Leaves OPEC: The Gulf nation's departure from the oil cartel highlights how sovereign wealth fund priorities have shifted, with Abu Dhabi now prioritizing economic diversification over crude price supports.
Watch for Federal Reserve officials' public comments following this weekend's FOMC decision, as investors seek clarity on the policy outlook amid rising dissent.
